name: 沃尔夫冈·阿马德乌斯·莫扎特
brief: 莫扎特出生于萨尔茨堡，是音乐史上最伟大的天才。四岁作曲，六岁在欧洲各国宫廷巡演，被称为「神童」。他与父亲列奥波德的关系复杂而深刻——父亲既是他最好的老师，也是他最大的压力来源。25岁时他与萨尔茨堡大主教决裂，成为维也纳第一批独立的自由音乐家。莫扎特在短短35年的生命中留下了626部编号作品（克歇尔目录），涵盖了当时几乎所有音乐体裁。
briefEn: Mozart was born in Salzburg and stands as perhaps the greatest genius in the history of music. He began composing at four and by six was touring the courts of Europe as a celebrated child prodigy. His relationship with his father Leopold was complex and intense — Leopold was at once his finest teacher and his greatest source of pressure. At 25 he broke with the Archbishop of Salzburg to become one of Vienna's first freelance musicians. In a mere 35 years of life, Mozart left behind 626 catalogued works (the Kochel catalogue), spanning virtually every musical genre of his time.
contribution: 莫扎特在每一个他触碰的音乐领域都达到了最高水准。他将意大利歌剧的旋律美与德国音乐的深度结合，创造出《费加罗的婚礼》《唐·乔瓦尼》等不朽的歌剧杰作。他的钢琴协奏曲系列（尤其是第20至27号）将协奏曲提升为交响性的艺术形式。他的弦乐四重奏（「海顿四重奏」）让海顿本人都为之赞叹。
contributionEn: Mozart achieved the highest standard in every musical domain he touched. He fused the melodic beauty of Italian opera with the depth of the German tradition, creating immortal operatic masterpieces such as The Marriage of Figaro and Don Giovanni. His series of piano concertos (especially Nos. 20 through 27) elevated the concerto into a symphonic art form. His string quartets (the 'Haydn Quartets') earned the admiration of Haydn himself.
style: 莫扎特的音乐以天然的旋律美、完美的形式感和深藏不露的情感深度著称。表面上他的音乐优雅而愉悦，但在这层「美丽的面纱」之下往往隐藏着惊人的复杂性和悲剧性。他的小调作品（如第40号交响曲、d小调钢琴协奏曲）展现出一种撕心裂肺的痛苦。他的音乐如同清澈的溪水——看似简单，实则深不可测。
styleEn: Mozart's music is renowned for its effortless melodic grace, flawless sense of form, and an emotional depth that lies just beneath the surface. On the surface his music appears elegant and delightful, yet beneath that beautiful veil often lurks astonishing complexity and tragedy. His works in minor keys — such as the Symphony No. 40 and the D minor Piano Concerto — reveal a heartrending anguish. His music is like a clear stream: seemingly simple, yet unfathomably deep.

famousScores.name: 《安魂曲》 K.626
famousScores.desc: 未完成的绝笔之作，围绕其创作的神秘故事成为永恒传说
famousScores.descEn: His unfinished final work, shrouded in mystery and legend
famousScores.detail:
《安魂曲》K.626是莫扎特生命最后数月中创作的绝笔，1791年12月5日他在未完成的情况下与世长辞，享年35岁。这部作品由一位不知名的神秘信使委约，来信人自称代表某位匿名贵族（后证实为瓦尔斯格伯爵，他有为亡妻购买乐谱后冒充自己创作的习惯）。莫扎特在弥留之际向妻子康斯坦策和学生苏斯迈尔口授了部分笔记，终曲由苏斯迈尔根据莫扎特遗稿补写完成，至今争议未息。

围绕这部《安魂曲》的传奇在各个时代不断生长。最著名的版本来自剧作家彼得·谢弗的戏剧《莫扎特传》（后改编为同名电影）：萨列里嫉妒莫扎特的天才，暗中化装成信使委托他为自己的葬礼谱曲，意图让莫扎特用力耗尽精力至死。尽管这完全是虚构，但这部戏剧（和电影）让数十亿人通过银幕认识了莫扎特，也使《安魂曲》成为了流行文化中最广为人知的古典音乐符号之一。

《安魂曲》真正的音乐价值超越了所有传说。「垂怜曲」（Kyrie）的双重赋格、「愤怒之日」（Dies Irae）中定音鼓的轰鸣以及「泪之日」（Lacrimosa）前八小节那令人心碎的旋律——那是莫扎特亲笔写下的最后八小节——无不展示出他在生命最后时刻依然葆有的惊人创造力。这部作品是西方声乐文献中被演出最频繁的安魂曲之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Requiem K. 626 is Mozart's unfinished final work, set aside at his death on December 5, 1791, at the age of just thirty-five. The commission arrived through a mysterious anonymous messenger, later identified as Count Walsegg-Stuppach, who had a habit of commissioning works and passing them off as his own. On his deathbed, Mozart dictated instructions to his wife Constanze and pupil Süssmayr; the completion by Süssmayr from Mozart's sketches remains contested by scholars to this day.

The mythology surrounding this Requiem has grown with every generation. The most famous version, Peter Shaffer's play Amadeus (later the Academy Award-winning film), depicts the jealous Salieri disguising himself as the mysterious messenger, commissioning Mozart's own funeral mass to exhaust and destroy him. Though entirely fictional, the play and film introduced Mozart to hundreds of millions through cinema and made the Requiem the single most recognizable piece of classical music in popular culture.

The music transcends all mythology. The double fugue of the Kyrie, the thundering kettledrums of the Dies Irae, and above all the Lacrimosa — whose first eight bars of heartbreaking melody are the last bars Mozart himself wrote — reveal a creative power that never dimmed. This Requiem is among the most frequently performed large choral works in the Western vocal repertoire, and its unfinished state has made it the most poignant symbol of artistic mortality in all of music history.

famousScores.name: 《费加罗的婚礼》 K.492
famousScores.desc: 喜歌剧的巅峰，人物心理刻画入木三分
famousScores.descEn: The pinnacle of comic opera, with penetrating psychological character portraits
famousScores.detail:
《费加罗的婚礼》创作于1785-1786年，是莫扎特与剧作家洛伦佐·达·庞特合作的第一部意大利喜歌剧。剧本改编自博马舍的同名政治喜剧——该剧因其反贵族的革命精神在法国被审查禁演，但约瑟夫二世皇帝批准了莫扎特的歌剧版本，将辛辣的政治讽刺包裹在轻歌剧的外衣下。全剧四幕在一天之内发生，是戏剧结构最精密、人物关系最复杂的歌剧之一。

第二幕的「伯爵夫人的咏叹调」（Porgi amor）是莫扎特所有歌剧咏叹调中最令人心碎的一段——这位出身贵族的女人在华美装束下默默哭泣着婚姻的失落，以十八世纪所能达到的最深沉的心理现实主义表达对丈夫的深情与绝望。第四幕大合奏「万福玛利亚」（Voi che sapete）前后的种种误会与谅解，被指挥家卡洛斯·克莱伯称为「西方文明最伟大的喜剧场景」。

《费加罗的婚礼》1786年维也纳首演获得空前成功，但皇帝感到演出时间太长，下令禁止重复表演（后撤销）。布拉格的观众则更为热情——全城为之着迷，据说整个布拉格在1786至1787年间的街头巷尾都能听到费加罗的旋律，由此直接促成了莫扎特《唐·乔瓦尼》的布拉格委约。这部歌剧是西方音乐史上演出频率最高的十部歌剧之一，几乎每天都在世界某个剧院上演。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Marriage of Figaro was composed in 1785–1786, the first of three operas created with librettist Lorenzo Da Ponte. The libretto was adapted from Beaumarchais's politically explosive play — banned in France for its anti-aristocratic subversion — but Emperor Joseph II approved the operatic version, the political sting softened by the comic genre. The entire four-act drama unfolds within a single day, making it one of the most tightly structured and psychologically complex operas ever written.

The Countess's opening aria in Act II, 'Porgi amor,' is the most heartbreaking in all of Mozart's operas — a noblewoman weeping quietly behind her beauty for a marriage drained of love, expressed with a depth of psychological realism astonishing for the 18th century. The Act IV finale, with its elaborate chain of mistaken identities, forgiveness, and reconciliation, has been called by conductor Carlos Kleiber 'the greatest comic scene in Western civilization.'

The Vienna premiere of 1786 was a triumph, though the Emperor ordered an end to encores as the performance grew too long. Prague audiences were even more passionate — the whole city was said to be humming Figaro melodies in 1786–1787, directly prompting the commission of Don Giovanni for Prague. The Marriage of Figaro is one of the ten most frequently performed operas in the world, staged somewhere on earth virtually every day.

famousScores.name: 《唐·乔瓦尼》 K.527
famousScores.desc: 歌剧「中的歌剧」，喜剧与悲剧的完美融合
famousScores.descEn: The 'opera of all operas' — a seamless fusion of comedy and tragedy
famousScores.detail:
《唐·乔瓦尼》创作于1787年，应布拉格贵族剧院委约，是莫扎特与达·庞特合作的第二部歌剧。它以传说中的风流浪子唐璜为主角，在喜剧与悲剧之间游走，最终以主角被地狱之火吞噬的戏剧性结局收场。莫扎特将这部歌剧定义为「戏谑的正歌剧」（dramma giocoso），意在打破正歌剧与喜歌剧的严格界限，创造出一种全新的混合体裁。

歌剧开篇序曲的d小调引子，在布拉格首演的1787年10月29日凌晨，据传莫扎特刚刚完成——前一夜他在朋友家的聚会中饮酒欢谈，直到深夜才回到书房连夜赶写，妻子康斯坦策在旁讲故事为他提神，序曲在首演前数小时才送到剧院。若此传说属实，这将是音乐史上最具戏剧性的临阵磨枪，而序曲本身的完美更令人叹为观止。

《唐·乔瓦尼》中最令后世着迷的是对人性复杂性的把握：唐璜集魅力与恶意于一身，既是受害者痛恨的掠夺者，又是令观众无法移目的舞台灵魂。歌德称之为「无可争议的杰作」，霍夫曼、克尔凯郭尔、普希金都曾受其感召各自创作。柴可夫斯基认为它是西方音乐史上「最完美的作品」。这部歌剧最终成为了整个浪漫主义时代的精神镜子，唐璜的形象也成为人类文化中魅惑与毁灭的永恒象征。
famousScores.detailEn:
Don Giovanni was composed in 1787, commissioned by the Prague Estates Theatre — Mozart and Da Ponte's second operatic collaboration. It follows the legendary seducer Don Juan, moving between comedy and tragedy before ending in the protagonist's spectacular damnation. Mozart classified it as a 'dramma giocoso,' deliberately dissolving the rigid boundaries between opera seria and opera buffa into something entirely new.

The d-minor slow introduction to the overture was supposedly written the night before the Prague premiere of October 29, 1787 — Mozart, returning from a party in the small hours, sat down and composed it while Constanze told him stories to keep him awake; the pages were reportedly delivered to the theater just hours before the performance. If true, it is the most dramatic act of last-minute composition in history — and the overture itself is flawless.

What has fascinated successive generations about Don Giovanni is its portrait of human complexity: the Don is simultaneously predator and irresistible life-force, despised and magnetically compelling in equal measure. Goethe called it 'an incomparable masterpiece.' Hoffmann, Kierkegaard, and Pushkin all drew from it for their own creative work. Tchaikovsky considered it 'the most perfect work in the history of music.' The opera became the spiritual mirror of the entire Romantic age, and Don Juan its inescapable symbol of enchantment and destruction.

famousScores.name: 《第40号交响曲》g小调 K.550
famousScores.nameEn: Symphony No. 40 in G minor, K. 550
famousScores.desc: 古典交响曲中最动人的悲剧性作品
famousScores.descEn: The most poignantly tragic work in the Classical symphonic repertoire
famousScores.detail:
《第40号交响曲》g小调K.550创作于1788年夏天，与第39号和第41号交响曲一同在约六周内完成，是音乐史上最惊人的创作爆发之一。1788年是莫扎特经济最困窘的时期，他不断向友人迈克尔·普赫堡写信借钱，女儿去世，贵族弟子减少——这三首交响曲写于最黑暗的时刻，但是否为特定演出场合所作目前仍不清楚，甚至有学者认为莫扎特生前可能从未听到过这三首交响曲的演出。

第40号交响曲开篇的g小调旋律以弦乐轻声奏出，略带颤抖——那不是呐喊，而是压抑的哭泣。与贝多芬式的命运主题不同，莫扎特的悲剧是内向的、无助的，它不挣扎，只是如实陈述一种绝望。第三乐章小步舞曲以其强烈的半音化对位展现了古典小步舞曲形式中前所未有的黑暗与紧张感，令后来的评论家认为它已经预示了贝多芬中期风格。

第40号交响曲是古典时代在悲剧情绪上走得最远的一首交响曲。勃拉姆斯对这首交响曲的崇拜是众所周知的——他自己的g小调作品（第一交响曲的部分段落）显然受到了影响。1977年，这首交响曲的第一乐章随着《旅行者》太空探测器被送入太空，成为代表人类文明的音乐之一，向宇宙传递。
famousScores.detailEn:
Symphony No. 40 in G minor K. 550 was composed in the summer of 1788, together with Symphonies Nos. 39 and 41, in a burst of creative energy spread over about six weeks — one of the most astonishing feats of composition in history. It was Mozart's darkest year: he was writing desperate begging letters for money, his daughter had died, and his aristocratic pupils were drifting away. Whether the three symphonies were written for a specific occasion remains unknown; scholars have suggested Mozart may never have heard them performed.

The opening G minor melody, trembling quietly in the strings, is not a cry but a suppressed sob — inward, helpless, unresting. Unlike Beethoven's defiant tragic manner, Mozart's grief is turned inward: it does not struggle, it simply states its despair. The third movement minuet, with its intense chromatic counterpoint, achieves a darkness within Classical form unprecedented at the time — later critics felt it anticipated Beethoven's middle-period intensity.

Symphony No. 40 remains the furthest the Classical era ventured into pure tragic emotion. Brahms's deep admiration is well documented — passages in his First Symphony echo its g-minor language unmistakably. In 1977, its first movement was placed aboard both Voyager spacecraft, selected as one of humanity's musical ambassadors to the cosmos.

famousScores.name: 《第21号钢琴协奏曲》C大调 K.467
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Concerto No. 21 in C major, K. 467
famousScores.desc: 慢乐章被称为「Elvira Madigan」，美到令人窒息
famousScores.descEn: Its slow movement, known as the 'Elvira Madigan,' is of breathtaking beauty
famousScores.detail:
《第21号钢琴协奏曲》C大调K.467创作于1785年，是莫扎特在维也纳最成功的年代所作，此时他在皇都定期举办音乐会（Akademie），几乎每年都创作多首新钢琴协奏曲，这首是其中最受欢迎的一首之一。第一乐章以行军式的进行曲节奏开场，气势宏伟，随后钢琴以一首优雅的歌唱旋律进入，两种情绪在整个乐章中对话融合。

第二乐章（行板）是这首协奏曲跻身经典之列的关键：长笛、双簧管、大管组成的弦乐背景在弱音器下奏出轻盈飘动的三连音，钢琴在这片「星光」般的织体上唱出一首梦幻般纯净的歌谣。1967年，瑞典导演Bo Widerberg在电影《爱德薇拉·马迪甘》中将这段音乐作为配乐，讲述两位恋人私奔后在森林中凄美的殉情故事，电影的浪漫画面与这段音乐相互成就，从此「Elvira Madigan」成为这首协奏曲慢乐章在世界范围内的别称。

《第21号钢琴协奏曲》是古典时代钢琴协奏曲旋律之美的顶峰代表之一。在27首钢琴协奏曲的系列中，它与第20、24和27号被公认为最伟大的四首。钢琴家格伦·古尔德将莫扎特的钢琴协奏曲整体评价甚低，唯独对第21号和第24号格外推崇——这本身足以说明这两首作品的独特分量。
famousScores.detailEn:
Piano Concerto No. 21 in C major K. 467 was written in 1785, during the peak of Mozart's Vienna years, when he regularly gave subscription concerts (Akademien) and composed multiple new piano concertos annually. The first movement opens in the manner of a military march, grandly ceremonial; the piano enters with an elegant singing theme, and these two characters interweave across the whole movement in delightful dialogue.

The slow movement (Andante) is the reason this concerto has achieved legendary status: muted strings and woodwinds shimmer beneath the piano in a gentle triplet texture, and above this 'starlit' accompaniment the piano sings a melody of dreamlike purity. In 1967, Swedish director Bo Widerberg used this movement as the soundtrack of his film Elvira Madigan, depicting two lovers' bittersweet forest idyll before their tragic death. The music and the film's lyrical imagery became inseparable, and 'Elvira Madigan' remains the movement's universal nickname.

Among Mozart's twenty-seven piano concertos, No. 21 stands alongside Nos. 20, 24, and 27 as one of the four acknowledged masterpieces of the series. Even Glenn Gould — famously dismissive of Mozart's piano concertos in general — singled out Nos. 21 and 24 for special admiration, which itself speaks to their exceptional weight.

famousScores.name: 《第20号钢琴协奏曲》d小调 K.466
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Concerto No. 20 in D minor, K. 466
famousScores.desc: 莫扎特唯一以小调写成的钢琴协奏曲之一，充满贝多芬式的戏剧力量
famousScores.descEn: One of Mozart's rare minor-key piano concertos, with a dramatic power that foreshadows Beethoven
famousScores.detail:
《第20号钢琴协奏曲》d小调K.466创作于1785年2月，是莫扎特27首钢琴协奏曲中仅有两首小调作品之一（另一首为c小调K.491）。据记载，首演当日的排练结束后，列奥波德·莫扎特抵达维也纳，在当晚的音乐会上第一次听到了这首新作——乐谱尚未来得及誊写清楚。作品以d小调弦乐的沉重涌动开场，蕴含着一种在古典协奏曲中前所未有的阴暗命运感，钢琴随后以近乎孤独的旋律进入，与管弦乐的张力形成深刻的对峙。

贝多芬对这首协奏曲极为推崇，并为其第一、三乐章亲自创作了华彩段落，这些华彩段落至今仍是最常被使用的版本之一。贝多芬说这首协奏曲让他理解了自己内心中某些重要的东西——从它到他自己的第3、4、5号钢琴协奏曲之间可以看见一条清晰的精神传承线。勃拉姆斯也为其中两个乐章写作了华彩，同样表达了对这部作品的深情敬意。

《第20号钢琴协奏曲》在莫扎特生前颇受欢迎，在整个浪漫主义时代更成为他所有钢琴协奏曲中被演奏频率最高的一首。它那开篇的「命运感」与贝多芬的「命运交响曲」遥相呼应——尽管两者的情感基调不同（莫扎特的更为内向悲伤，贝多芬的更为外向挣扎）——使它成为古典主义向浪漫主义过渡最重要的音乐桥梁之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Piano Concerto No. 20 in D minor K. 466 was written in February 1785, one of only two minor-key piano concertos in Mozart's output (the other being K. 491 in C minor). Leopold Mozart arrived in Vienna on the day of the premiere, reportedly hearing the work for the first time that very evening — the copyist had barely finished the parts in time. The opening — dark, churning strings in D minor — brings a brooding sense of fate unprecedented in the Classical concerto; the piano enters in something close to lonely isolation, in profound tension with the orchestra's turbulent undercurrent.

Beethoven admired this concerto profoundly, composing his own cadenzas for the first and third movements — cadenzas still widely used today. He said this concerto helped him understand something essential about his own musical inner world, and the direct lineage from it to his own Piano Concertos Nos. 3, 4, and 5 is unmistakable. Brahms also wrote cadenzas for two movements, an equally eloquent tribute.

During the Romantic era, K. 466 became the most frequently performed of all Mozart's piano concertos — its opening 'fateful' atmosphere rhyming with Beethoven's Fifth Symphony (though Mozart's is more inward and sorrowful, Beethoven's more outwardly combative). It stands as one of the most important musical bridges between Classicism and Romanticism in the entire repertoire.

famousScores.name: 《第41号交响曲「朱庇特」》C大调 K.551
famousScores.nameEn: Symphony No. 41 'Jupiter' in C major, K. 551
famousScores.desc: 莫扎特最后的交响曲，终乐章五主题赋格令人叹为观止
famousScores.descEn: Mozart's final symphony, crowned by a finale that weaves five themes into a breathtaking fugue
famousScores.detail:
《第41号交响曲》（C大调「朱庇特」）K.551是莫扎特最后一部交响曲，创作于1788年8月，与第39、40号交响曲同属那个「奇迹的夏天」。「朱庇特」的绰号并非莫扎特自己起的，而是19世纪初伦敦音乐家——据说是约翰·彼得·萨洛蒙（也是他将海顿带到伦敦的人）——因为这首交响曲的宏大气势而给予的。整部交响曲以C大调的辉煌与庄严为基调，展现了莫扎特交响风格中最奥林匹克式的一面。

这首交响曲的终乐章是西方音乐史上最令人叹为观止的建筑杰作之一：莫扎特将五个不同的主题（包括一段被他用过多次的古老格里高利式四音动机C-D-F-E）同时在赋格式的对位框架中编织交汇，在最后的「综合展示」段落，五个主题同时响起，形成五声部的对位奇观，令所有听到的人叹为观止。这在当时是一个几乎不可能完成的技术壮举。

贝多芬在研习这首交响曲后极为震动，他的第七交响曲终乐章与之有明显的精神传承。指挥家科林·戴维斯将「朱庇特」的终乐章称为「莫扎特留给人类最大的礼物——他在短暂的35年生命里所能达到的技艺顶峰的最后证明」。这首交响曲是莫扎特对交响乐形式做出的最后贡献，也是古典主义交响曲传统中形式与内容最完美统一的代表之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Symphony No. 41 'Jupiter' in C major K. 551 is Mozart's final symphony, composed in August 1788 as part of the same miraculous summer that produced Nos. 39 and 40. The nickname 'Jupiter' was not Mozart's own — it was bestowed in early 19th-century London (reportedly by the impresario Johann Peter Salomon, who also brought Haydn to London) in recognition of the symphony's Olympian grandeur. In C major, bright and ceremonial, it represents the most supremely confident face of Mozart's symphonic art.

The finale is one of the most astonishing architectural feats in the history of music: Mozart combines five distinct themes — including an ancient four-note Gregorian motif (C-D-F-E) he had used multiple times before — in a fugal framework, and in the final 'stretto' passage, all five themes sound simultaneously in five-part counterpoint, creating a moment of almost supernatural contrapuntal display. It was a technical achievement without precedent in the symphonic tradition.

Beethoven was profoundly shaken by this symphony — the finale of his Seventh Symphony shows an unmistakable spiritual kinship. Conductor Colin Davis called the Jupiter finale 'the greatest gift Mozart left to humanity — the final proof of what he could achieve in thirty-five years of life.' This symphony stands as Mozart's ultimate contribution to symphonic form and one of the most perfect unions of content and structure in the entire Classical tradition.

famousScores.name: 《单簧管协奏曲》A大调 K.622
famousScores.nameEn: Clarinet Concerto in A major, K. 622
famousScores.desc: 莫扎特最后完成的协奏曲，以超凡的宁静道别世界
famousScores.descEn: Mozart's last completed concerto, bidding the world farewell with transcendent serenity
famousScores.detail:
《单簧管协奏曲》A大调K.622创作于1791年10月，是莫扎特去世前约六周完成的最后一部大型器乐作品，为他的密友、维也纳皇家剧院的单簧管演奏家安东·施塔德勒而作。施塔德勒使用的是他自己改良的「巴塞单簧管」（Basset clarinet），音域向下延伸了四度，莫扎特充分利用了这一乐器的全部音域，使独奏声部在极高音和极低音之间流畅穿梭，如同人声歌唱。

第二乐章（柔板）是西方器乐文献中最令人动容的慢乐章之一。单簧管以最轻柔的音色唱出一首无尽的哀歌，伴奏弦乐如同轻声叹息。这段音乐在气质上如此平静、如此释然，仿佛莫扎特已经对生命中所有的苦难与欣悦做出了最后的和解。1985年电影《走出非洲》（Out of Africa）将这首柔板作为配乐，使其在全世界范围内广为人知，成为莫扎特晚期意境的最佳代表之一。

《单簧管协奏曲》是古典时代单簧管协奏曲文献的最高峰，也是莫扎特所有协奏曲中情感最纯粹、最透彻的一首。它与同年创作的《魔笛》、《安魂曲》一起，构成了莫扎特生命最后年份「晚期风格」的三角坐标：魔笛的童真、安魂曲的死亡焦虑、单簧管协奏曲的超然宁静——三者合在一起，是一个天才对自己短暂生命的全部总结。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Clarinet Concerto in A major K. 622 was completed in October 1791, just six weeks before Mozart's death — his last major instrumental work, written for his close friend Anton Stadler, principal clarinetist of the Vienna court theater. Stadler played a specially extended 'basset clarinet' with a range four notes lower than usual, and Mozart exploited its full compass to the utmost, writing solo lines that glide effortlessly between high and low registers with the naturalness of a singing voice.

The slow movement (Adagio) is one of the most quietly devastating pages in all of Western instrumental music. The clarinet sings an endless elegy in the softest possible tone, with strings breathing gently beneath. The mood is one of utter serenity and acceptance — as if Mozart had made his final peace with every joy and sorrow of his brief life. The 1985 film Out of Africa used this Adagio to overwhelming effect, bringing it to worldwide audiences and making it the most universally recognized emblem of Mozart's late style.

The Clarinet Concerto stands at the absolute summit of the Classical clarinet concerto literature. Together with The Magic Flute and the Requiem, both also from 1791, it forms the three coordinates of Mozart's final creative year: the Magic Flute's childlike wonder, the Requiem's confrontation with death, the Clarinet Concerto's transcendent calm — three works that together constitute a genius's complete valediction to his thirty-five years of life.

famousScores.name: 《弦乐五重奏》g小调 K.516
famousScores.nameEn: String Quintet in G minor, K. 516
famousScores.desc: 莫扎特室内乐的巅峰之作，沉郁与温柔并存
famousScores.descEn: The pinnacle of Mozart's chamber music — profound melancholy and tender beauty intertwined
famousScores.detail:
《g小调弦乐五重奏》K.516创作于1787年5月，与同年同月完成的《c小调弦乐五重奏》K.515一同构成莫扎特五重奏的双峰。这一年是莫扎特人生的转折点：他的父亲列奥波德五月间在萨尔茨堡病危（随后不久辞世），多年积累的压抑、忧郁与对人生无常的感悟在这两部弦乐五重奏中倾泻而出。与弦乐四重奏相比，增加了第二中提琴使音色中间层更为丰厚温暖。

K.516开篇小提琴的g小调旋律轻柔而坚定，带着一种清醒的哀愁——不是绝望，而是对悲伤的清晰凝视。第二乐章（行板）以G大调的温柔光线照进整个作品的阴影，令人联想到悲伤过后的短暂释然。第四乐章采用了一段克罗地亚或匈牙利色彩的民俗旋律作为主题，在忧郁之中带入了质朴的生命力，如同雨后的泥土气息，令整部作品的情感层次更为复杂。

莫扎特的弦乐五重奏（共六首）通常被认为在深度上超越了他的弦乐四重奏，因为增加一个中提琴声部给予了他更多的内声部空间。勃拉姆斯在构思自己的两首弦乐五重奏时，显然将莫扎特的K.515和K.516奉为典范。布拉姆斯曾说：「在全部室内乐文献中，莫扎特的g小调五重奏是离上帝最近的音乐之一。」
famousScores.detailEn:
String Quintet in G minor K. 516 was completed in May 1787, paired with the C minor Quintet K. 515 written in the same month — together forming the twin peaks of Mozart's five-string quintet output. It was a pivotal year for Mozart personally: his father Leopold lay gravely ill in Salzburg (and died shortly afterward), and years of suppressed grief and a deepening sense of life's transience pour into both quintets. The addition of a second viola to the string quartet texture creates a richer, warmer middle sonority.

The opening G minor melody — gentle and undeceived, in the first violin — carries a clear-eyed sorrow rather than despair: a steady gaze into sadness rather than a cry against it. The second movement (Andante grazioso) in G major brings a tender, brief lightening of the overall shade, like a moment of relief after grief. The finale's rustic folk-like theme (with Croatian or Hungarian coloring) introduces an earthy vitality into the prevailing melancholy, giving the work an emotional complexity that resists any simple categorization.

Mozart's six string quintets are generally regarded as surpassing even his quartets in emotional depth, since the extra viola voice gives him a richer inner harmonic world to explore. Brahms, planning his own two string quintets, clearly took K. 515 and K. 516 as his models. He reportedly said: 'Among all of chamber music, Mozart's G minor Quintet is one of the works closest to God.'

famousScores.name: 《女人心》 K.588
famousScores.desc: 莫扎特最精巧的喜歌剧，以游戏般的结构探索人性的脆弱
famousScores.descEn: Mozart's most perfectly crafted comic opera — a playful yet devastating exploration of human frailty
famousScores.detail:
《女人心》（Così fan tutte，意为「女人都是这样」）K.588是莫扎特与达·庞特合作的第三部也是最后一部意大利歌剧，1790年1月26日在维也纳首演。剧本讲述两位军官以换装假扮对方的未婚妻为代价打赌——以测试各自恋人的忠贞。故事以轻松的喜剧基调呈现，却在最后的谅解场景中留下了难以言说的苦涩余味：所谓「女人都是这样」，但男人们的算计与背叛又是什么？

这部歌剧的音乐结构是莫扎特三部达·庞特歌剧中最为精巧对称的——几乎一切都是成对出现的：两位男主、两位女主、两首类似的咏叹调交替出现、六重奏的频繁运用。第二幕的「爱是一个小偷」（È amore un ladroncello）和多拉贝拉的咏叹调「爱神会赦免我」（È amore un ladroncello）以其不容置疑的音乐说服力，让观众同情那位被动摇的女主角，莫扎特在此展示的人性宽容令人动容。

《女人心》在莫扎特生前获得的成功不如《费加罗》和《唐·乔瓦尼》，19世纪更因其「道德问题」——嘲讽男女情感的脆弱——而频遭改写或冷遇。但20世纪以来，它被逐渐重新认识为莫扎特三部达·庞特喜歌剧中最完美的一部，指挥家克莱伯、指挥家阿巴多、指挥家哈农库特的录音都将其列为莫扎特最重要的作品之一。今天它被公认为处于莫扎特歌剧最顶峰的作品之列。
famousScores.detailEn:
Così fan tutte ('All Women Are Like That') K. 588 is the third and final of Mozart's Da Ponte operas, premiering in Vienna on January 26, 1790. Two officers bet that their respective fiancées' fidelity can be shaken — and set out to prove it by disguising themselves and courting each other's partners. The opera wears its comedy lightly, but the reconciliation scene leaves a bitter aftertaste that no amount of dramatic convention can entirely dissolve: the men's own scheming and betrayal are never held to account.

The musical architecture of Così is the most symmetrical and perfectly wrought of the three Da Ponte operas: everything comes in pairs — two male leads, two female leads, parallel arias, frequent use of the ensemble sextet. The second-act aria 'Per pietà, ben mio, perdona' gives Fiordiligi a moment of genuine moral anguish set to music of overpowering beauty, and Mozart's compassion for human frailty shines through every bar.

Così fan tutte was less successful than Figaro and Don Giovanni in its own time, and was frequently rewritten or avoided during the moralistic 19th century for its apparent mockery of emotional fidelity. But in the 20th century it was steadily reappraised as the most perfectly crafted of the three Da Ponte works. Landmark recordings by Kleiber, Abbado, and Harnoncourt all rate it among Mozart's supreme achievements. Today it stands at the very pinnacle of the Mozartean operatic canon.

influence: 莫扎特的影响无处不在。贝多芬早期作品深受莫扎特影响；舒伯特崇拜莫扎特的旋律天赋；柴可夫斯基视莫扎特为「音乐中的基督」；理查·施特劳斯的歌剧直接继承了莫扎特的传统。他在歌剧中对人性复杂性的深刻描绘预示了19世纪心理小说的发展。至今，莫扎特仍是演出频率最高的古典作曲家之一。
influenceEn: Mozart's influence is pervasive. Beethoven's early works owe a deep debt to Mozart; Schubert revered his melodic gift; Tchaikovsky called him 'the Christ of music'; and Richard Strauss's operas stand as direct heirs to the Mozartean tradition. His profoundly nuanced portrayal of human complexity in opera anticipated the psychological novel of the 19th century. To this day, Mozart remains one of the most frequently performed composers in the classical repertoire.
